items.q: What is hydraulic action in a river?
items.correct: The force of moving water itself compressing air into cracks in the river bank or bed, weakening rock until pieces break off — no sediment needed.
items.misconception: Hydraulic action is when rocks in the river smash into each other.
items.random_wrong: Hydraulic action is when the river dissolves rocks chemically.
items.why: Students mix up the four river erosion processes. Hydraulic action = water force; abrasion = water using rocks to scrape; attrition = rocks hitting rocks; solution (corrosion) = chemical dissolving.

items.correct: When stressed (usually by warming), corals expel their symbiotic zooxanthellae (algae that provide colour and food), turning white. They can recover if stress eases, but die if it persists.
items.misconception: Coral bleaching is when coral is killed by sunlight bleaching its colour.
items.random_wrong: Coral bleaching is when boats spill chemicals that whiten the reef.
items.why: It's the loss of algal symbionts, not sun-fading. Bleached coral is not yet dead — it's a warning state. Repeated mass bleaching (like 2016 on the Great Barrier Reef) causes long-term reef collapse.

items.correct: The area of land drained by a river and its tributaries, bounded by a watershed (high ground that separates one basin from another).
items.misconception: A drainage basin is the bowl-shaped area at the mouth of the river.
items.random_wrong: A drainage basin is the underground source of a river.
items.why: Students take 'basin' too literally as a bowl. It is the entire catchment area — often a huge region, not a depression near the river itself.
The answer examiners want is: Hess. “Wegener” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: What was the Tohoku earthquake magnitude?
items.why: Correct answer: 9.0. “7.0” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: How tall (m) was the Tohoku tsunami?
items.why: The real figure is 15 m. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
